title: 2-Thiopyrogallol : A New Spectrophotometric Reagent for Rhenium
author: SASWATI P. BAG
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Jadavpur Univershy, Calcutta-700 032 
  

  Manuscript received 19 February 1980, revised 30 June 1983, accepted 3 December 1983  

 2-Thiopyrogallol reacts with rhenium(V) to give a violet colour complex formed by reducing ReO \(O^-_4\)  with the ligand itself at 9  N  HCl. The complex has absorption maximum at 535 nm and is extractable into a mixture of   \({iso}\)  -amyl alcohol and chloroform (1: 9). Beer's law is obeyed from 0.25 to 16 ppm rhenium ( \(\varepsilon\) =1.58x10 4 ). With the exception of molybdenum and palladium, the method is free from usual interferences. The isolated complex has the composition [Re(LH) 2 (LH 2 )(H 2 O)] this is supported by elemental analysis, ir. conductance, ion-exchange and magnetic moment studies. By proper extension of Leden's method, the overall as well as stepwise formation constants for 1 : 3 system have been evaluated.

The complex has absorption maximum at 535 nm and is extractable into a mixture of   \({iso}\)  -amyl alcohol and chloroform (1: 9). Beer's law is obeyed from 0.25 to 16 ppm rhenium ( \(\varepsilon\) =1.58x10 4 ). With the exception of molybdenum and palladium, the method is free from usual interferences. The isolated complex has the composition [Re(LH) 2 (LH 2 )(H 2 O)] this is supported by elemental analysis, ir. conductance, ion-exchange and magnetic moment studies. By proper extension of Leden's method, the overall as well as stepwise formation constants for 1 : 3 system have been evaluated.
